gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Whether `node` is a **provably pure** (effect-free) Core IR fragment — the structural,"] # [doc = " conservative, EXPLAIN-able gate. See the module docs for the grounding of each case."] # [doc = " All-or-nothing over the whole subtree: a single impure/opaque leaf (a `wild:` op, or any `Swap`)"] # [doc = " makes the **entire** fragment ineligible, never just the leaf."] # [must_use] pub fn is_pure (node : & Node) -> bool { let mut work : Vec < & Node > = vec ! [node] ; while let Some (n) = work . pop () { match n { Node :: Const (_) | Node :: Var (_) | Node :: Lam { .. } => { } Node :: Op { prim , args } => { if prim . starts_with ("wild:") { return false ; } work . extend (args . iter ()) ; } Node :: Swap { .. } => return false , Node :: Let { bound , body , .. } => { work . push (bound) ; work . push (body) ; } Node :: Construct { args , .. } => work . extend (args . iter ()) , Node :: Match { scrutinee , alts , default , } => { work . push (scrutinee) ; for a in alts { match a { Alt :: Ctor { body , .. } | Alt :: Lit { body , .. } => work . push (body) , } } if let Some (d) = default . as_deref () { work . push (d) ; } } Node :: App { func , arg } => { work . push (func) ; work . push (arg) ; } Node :: Fix { body , .. } => work . push (body) , Node :: FixGroup { defs , body } => { for (_ , d) in defs { work . push (d) ; } work . push (body) ; } } } true }

A"] # [doc = " caller/tool can ask *exactly* what the parallel evaluator intends to do before it runs."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum ParallelPlan { # [doc = " The fragment is **impure** (a `wild:` op or a `Swap` appears somewhere) — evaluated wholly"] # [doc = " sequentially by [`Interpreter::eval_core`], never reordered."] SequentialImpure , # [doc = " The fragment is pure but its **outermost node is not an independent-argument batch worth"] # [doc = " parallelizing** (fewer than two top-level arguments, or a `Let`/`Match`/`App`/`Fix`/… head) —"] # [doc = " evaluated wholly sequentially by [`Interpreter::eval_core`]."] SequentialNoBatch , # [doc = " The outermost node is a pure `Op`/`Construct` with **≥2 independent arguments**; those"] # [doc = " arguments are the batch dispatched across [`Scheduler::run_indexed`] (a single, non-nested"] # [doc = " fan-out), each evaluated sequentially within its worker."] TopLevelBatch { # [doc = " Which node family heads the batch."] head : BatchHead , # [doc = " The batch width (number of independent arguments fanned out)."] width : usize , } , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Compute the [`ParallelPlan`] for `node` — the explicit, side-effect-free decision procedure"] # [doc = " [`Interpreter::eval_core_parallel`] follows (and that a caller can inspect up front). Looks at the"] # [doc = " **outermost node only** (the interim top-level bound; see module docs)."] # [must_use] pub fn plan_parallel (node : & Node) -> ParallelPlan { if ! is_pure (node) { return ParallelPlan :: SequentialImpure ; } match node { Node :: Op { args , .. } if args . len () >= MIN_BATCH_WIDTH => ParallelPlan :: TopLevelBatch { head : BatchHead :: Op , width : args . len () , } , Node :: Construct { args , .. } if args . len () >= MIN_BATCH_WIDTH => { ParallelPlan :: TopLevelBatch { head : BatchHead :: Construct , width : args . len () , } } _ => ParallelPlan :: SequentialNoBatch , } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Tick the shared fuel counter (an [`AtomicU64`] so concurrent batch workers share one budget),"] # [doc = " returning [`EvalError::FuelExhausted`] on underflow — never silent, and never a per-worker budget"] # [doc = " that could let two threads jointly overrun the declared total (RFC-0007 §4.5 CakeML clock)."] fn tick (fuel : & AtomicU64) -> Result < () , EvalError > { fuel . fetch_update (Ordering :: Relaxed , Ordering :: Relaxed , | f | f . checked_sub (1)) . map (| _ | ()) . map_err (| _ | EvalError :: FuelExhausted) }

gaps.snippet: impl Interpreter { # [doc = " Evaluate `node` to a [`CoreValue`] with the **same result** as [`Interpreter::eval_core`],"] # [doc = " except that when the fragment's [`plan_parallel`] is a [`ParallelPlan::TopLevelBatch`] the"] # [doc = " outermost independent argument batch is evaluated **in parallel** on the M-861"] # [doc = " [`Scheduler`] — a single, non-nested [`Scheduler::run_indexed`] fan-out (the interim"] # [doc = " top-level bound; see module docs). Observable behaviour is identical to the sequential"] # [doc = " reference (RT2-preserving): [`Scheduler::run_indexed`] returns outputs in **spawn order**, so"] # [doc = " the result is deterministic regardless of the steal schedule, and each argument is reduced by"] # [doc = " the trusted sequential [`Interpreter::step`]. Any fragment that is impure, or whose outermost"] # [doc = " node is not a ≥2-argument `Op`/`Construct`, falls back **wholesale** to"] # [doc = " [`Interpreter::eval_core`] (never a partial/mixed order — G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Empirical, differential-checked** (M-862): `eval_core_parallel(e) == eval_core(e)` for `e`"] # [doc = " in the pure fragment is checked over a corpus in `src/tests/parallel.rs`, not proven."] pub fn eval_core_parallel (& self , node : & Node) -> Result < CoreValue , EvalError > { match plan_parallel (node) { ParallelPlan :: SequentialImpure | ParallelPlan :: SequentialNoBatch => { self . eval_core (node) } ParallelPlan :: TopLevelBatch { head , .. } => self . eval_top_batch (node , head) , } } # [doc = " Evaluate `node` to a representation [`crate::Value`], mirroring [`Interpreter::eval`] — see"] # [doc = " [`Interpreter::eval_core_parallel`] for the parallel-evaluation contract."] pub fn eval_parallel (& self , node : & Node) -> Result < crate :: Value , EvalError > { match self . eval_core_parallel (node) ? { CoreValue :: Repr (v) => Ok (v) , CoreValue :: Data (_) => Err (EvalError :: DataResult) , } } # [doc = " Fan the outermost `Op`/`Construct` argument batch across the scheduler (a single, non-nested"] # [doc = " `run_indexed`), then recombine. Each argument is reduced to a normal-form [`Node`] by the"] # [doc = " trusted sequential small-step interpreter ([`Interpreter::eval_to_normal_node`]) inside its"] # [doc = " worker — so this introduces **no** second semantics, only scheduling. Only ever called for a"] # [doc = " [`ParallelPlan::TopLevelBatch`] node (a pure `Op`/`Construct` with ≥2 args)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Exact-equivalence discipline (fixes a fuel-starvation divergence, M-862 follow-up)"] # [doc = " The trusted sequential `eval_core`/`step` is strict left-to-right and **short-circuits**: it"] # [doc = " stops at the first erroring argument and never spends fuel on the ones after it. Dispatching"] # [doc = " every argument as a concurrent job against one shared fuel counter breaks that: a"] # [doc = " fuel-hungry/non-terminating sibling can drain the shared budget and starve an *earlier* arg"] # [doc = " into `FuelExhausted` where the sequential reference would have reached a deterministic"] # [doc = " non-fuel error first (or vice versa) — schedule-dependent, so it can even disagree with"] # [doc = " itself run to run."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The fix keeps the parallel speedup exactly where it is safe and defers to the reference"] # [doc = " everywhere it might not be:"] # [doc = " - Snapshot `F0 = self.fuel` and run the batch on a **separate** clone of it (never the real"] # [doc = "   counter — there is nothing to corrupt if the attempt is discarded)."] # [doc = " - If **every** argument job returns `Ok` (the clone was never exhausted and no argument"] # [doc = "   errored), the parallel run cannot have diverged: sequential would have run the same"] # [doc = "   arguments to the same values, spending the same total fuel (order-independent for a simple"] # [doc = "   decrement-by-1 pool once every request succeeds). Commit that result."] # [doc = " - If **any** argument job returns `Err` (including `FuelExhausted`) the parallel attempt may"] # [doc = "   have evaluated (or starved) siblings the sequential reference would never have reached —"] # [doc = "   **discard it entirely** (never commit its partially-consumed fuel) and re-evaluate the whole"] # [doc = "   node via the trusted sequential [`Interpreter::eval_core`] on a fresh `F0`. That is the"] # [doc = "   authoritative, deterministic answer."] fn eval_top_batch (& self , node : & Node , head : BatchHead) -> Result < CoreValue , EvalError > { let args : & [Node] = match node { Node :: Op { args , .. } | Node :: Construct { args , .. } => args , _ => { return Err (EvalError :: DataMalformed { why : "eval_top_batch reached a non-Op/Construct head" . to_owned () , }) } } ; let fuel = Arc :: new (AtomicU64 :: new (self . fuel)) ; let interp = Arc :: new (self . clone ()) ; let jobs : Vec < _ > = args . iter () . map (| arg | { let interp = Arc :: clone (& interp) ; let fuel = Arc :: clone (& fuel) ; let arg = arg . clone () ; move | | interp . eval_to_normal_node (& arg , & fuel) }) . collect () ; let results : Vec < Result < Node , EvalError > > = Scheduler :: new () . run_indexed (jobs , None , None) ; if results . iter () . any (Result :: is_err) { return self . eval_core (node) ; } let normals : Vec < Node > = results . into_iter () . map (| r | r . expect ("checked all Ok above")) . collect () ; match head { BatchHead :: Op => { let prim = match node { Node :: Op { prim , .. } => prim , _ => unreachable ! ("head == Op ⇒ node is Op") , } ; let values = collect_values (& normals) ? ; let f = self . prims . get (prim) . ok_or_else (| | EvalError :: UnknownPrim (prim . clone ())) ? ; let result = f (prim , & values) ? ; tick (& fuel) ? ; Ok (CoreValue :: Repr (result)) } BatchHead :: Construct => { let ctor = match node { Node :: Construct { ctor , .. } => ctor , _ => unreachable ! ("head == Construct ⇒ node is Construct") , } ; let rebuilt = Node :: Construct { ctor : ctor . clone () , args : normals , } ; node_to_core_value (& rebuilt , & RecursionBudget :: default ()) } } } # [doc = " Reduce `node` to a **normal-form [`Node`]** (a `Const`, or a saturated `Construct` of values)"] # [doc = " by iterating the trusted sequential small-step [`Interpreter::step`] under the shared `fuel`"] # [doc = " clock. This is the exact [`Interpreter::eval_core`] loop, returning the normal-form node"] # [doc = " instead of reading it off — so a batch worker uses the trusted semantics verbatim, never a"] # [doc = " reimplementation. Runs entirely on one thread (no nested parallelism)."] fn eval_to_normal_node (& self , node : & Node , fuel : & AtomicU64) -> Result < Node , EvalError > { let sizing = RecursionBudget :: default () ; ensure_sufficient_stack (& sizing , move | | { let mut current = node . clone () ; loop { match self . step (& current) ? { Step :: Value => return Ok (current) , Step :: Next (next) => { tick (fuel) ? ; current = * next ; } } } }) } }
